title: LLM Communication Boundary Card
description: A compact structure for locking evaluation frames and dialogue boundaries before generating or delivering LLM output.

user_goal.description: The user's real goal or decision context for the answer.

evaluator.description: The person, audience, institution, or standard that evaluates whether the output is successful.

success_standard.description: The standard used to judge meaning, value, quality, or usefulness.

cost_model.description: The expected cost, rigor, time, evidence, and implementation burden assumed by the answer.

speaker.description: The voice or role that final text should use.

recipient.description: The person, audience, tool, or downstream agent that will receive the final output.

output_type.description: The output contract that determines what may appear in the final text.

visible_context.description: Facts and context the recipient is allowed to see.

internal_context.description: Upstream instructions, private goals, corrections, negotiations, or reasoning that must stay internal.

forbidden_context.description: Specific content that must not appear in the final output.

tone.description: The desired tone as it should appear to the recipient, without exposing upstream tone negotiation.

must_include.description: Recipient-visible points that must be included.

must_not_include.description: Points, phrases, standards, or layers that must be excluded.

leakage_risks.description: Boundary failure risks to check before returning final output.

instruction_drift_risks.description: Risks that a generated instruction, skill, policy, paper section, or task has drifted from the original concept.
